Section 1  
The function analyzes the health improvements reported by participants in different age groups.  

Section 2  
The function processes a list of dictionaries containing age group and health improvements.  

Section 3  
The function counts the frequency of each health improvement for each age group.  

Section 4  
The function returns a dictionary with the most common health improvement for each age group.  

Section 5  
The function ensures that the output is a dictionary with the most common improvement per age group.  

Section 6  
The function handles multiple participants and aggregates their improvements.  

Section 7  
The function is designed to be efficient and easy to understand for users of all skill levels.  

* The function processes the input data correctly.  
* The function returns the expected output as specified.
